title: Logs are processing
description: Logging personal data is collection, processing, retention, and possible disclosure, not a harmless debug side effect.
body: Use when adding or inspecting logs, traces, metrics, crash reports, analytics events, prompt transcripts, or support bundles. Do not assume internal-only logs are safe. Actions: classify fields before logging, redact at producer, set retention boundaries, and avoid high-cardinality personal labels. Validation signals include redacted log snapshots and no personal field names in metrics labels. Risk boundary: production logs containing raw sensitive data require human incident review. Use when Privacy Risk Controls work touches personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
